purpose: Portal 1-style terminal layout reused for the Portal 2 "Want You Gone" alternate page. It positions lyric and credit boxes and includes leftover picture-region styling. Surfaces a :root --text-color CSS custom property (default #ff0 yellow) so portal2/portal1style/cake.js applyTextColor can override the page color via a hex string. Also defines the .color-cycle keyframe animation (hue-rotate 60s loop, suppressed by prefers-reduced-motion) and the body.color-rainbow > #lyricstext/#creditstext > *:nth-child(6n+k) palette (yellow / amber / red / magenta / cyan / mint) that recolors lyric and credit spans cyclically — identical to portal/style.css.
invariants: Dimensions align with the hard-coded text border counts in portal2/portal1style/cake.js.
The lyric and credit containers remain fixed-position boxes, matching the legacy Portal 1 look.

:root --text-color is the contract surface for A5 text-color modes; body color resolves through this var, so portal2/portal1style/cake.js applyTextColor() can swap the page color by setting it on document.documentElement.style. Default #ff0 = original 'yellow' keyword.
.color-cycle and .color-rainbow are mutually exclusive body classes — A5 launcher writes one or the other (never both).
Rainbow palette is 6 colors (yellow / amber / red / magenta / cyan / mint) repeated via :nth-child(6n+k); rules apply ONLY to direct children of #lyricstext and #creditstext.
gotchas: #picture styling is present even though portal2/portal1style/index.html does not declare a picture element.
Hue-rotate on body.color-cycle affects EVERYTHING inside body (borders included) — intentional whole-page hue cycling.
Rules are duplicated between portal/style.css and this file; if the palette or animation duration is ever tuned, both files need to stay in lockstep.
